description: Rapid workflow for bug fixes, chores, and small tasks. Diagnose before fixing, verify the outcome, leave a regression test.

title.description: What needs to be done (short description)

steps.title: Diagnose: {{title}}
steps.description:
Phase: UNDERSTAND

Before fixing anything, understand the root cause.

1. Reproduce the problem — run the failing command, see the actual error
2. Trace the cause — don't stop at the symptom. Follow the error to its source.
   - Is this a direct bug, or a symptom of something upstream?
   - Are there related failures that share the same root cause? (check bd list for siblings)
   - Would fixing this fix other open beads too?
3. Write a one-sentence root cause: 'This fails because [X], not because [surface symptom].'
4. Update this bead's description with the root cause finding.

If diagnosis reveals this is bigger than expected (multiple root causes, architectural issue, needs design), recommend upgrading to mol-feature instead of continuing.

Close this step when root cause is identified.

steps.title: Fix: {{title}}
steps.description:
Phase: FIX

Implement the fix with TDD:
1. Write a failing test that reproduces the bug (the test must fail WITHOUT the fix and pass WITH it)
2. Implement the minimal fix
3. Run the test — verify it passes
4. Check for downstream effects — did this fix unblock other failing tests/models?

The regression test is NOT optional. Every bug fix must leave a test that would catch the bug if it recurred. Tag the test with the requirement ID: // covers #{{title}}-regression

Acceptance: {{acceptance}}

steps.title: Verify: {{title}}
steps.description:
Phase: CONFIRM

Verify the OUTCOME, not just that tests pass:
1. Run the exact workflow the user cares about — not just the unit test
2. Check downstream: did fixing this resolve related failures? Update or close sibling beads if so.
3. Check the acceptance criterion: {{acceptance}} — is it actually true now?
4. Dispatch adversarial-reviewer subagent (isolated, no conversation history) on the changed files — even for bugs, the fix should be reviewed.

If the outcome isn't achieved, keep working. 'Tests pass' is not verification.
